expectations.evidence: Formatted '## Trade-off Matrix' table with 8 quality attributes as rows (Security/HIPAA, Simplicity, Time-to-Market, Scalability, Deployability, Fault Isolation, Maintainability, Cost) and 3 options as columns.
3 options analyzed: (1) Microservices, (2) Monolith (Modular), (3) Service-Based Architecture.
Explicitly names driving quality attributes: Security/Compliance, Simplicity/Time-to-Market, Scalability. Matrix adds: Deployability, Fault Isolation, Maintainability, Cost.
Clear recommendation: 'Modular Monolith' with 4-point justification covering HIPAA compliance surface, team capacity, scalability trade-off acceptance, and time-to-market.
Section 'Explicit trade-offs being accepted' lists: all-or-nothing scaling, single point of failure (with mitigation), module boundary erosion risk (with mitigation).
Recommendation explicitly states: 'Modular Monolith -- the least worst choice for this context because:'
Analysis directly references: 3-person development team, HIPAA compliance as non-negotiable, CTO vs lead developer disagreement, greenfield SaaS, healthcare domain.
Microservices has 5 detailed negatives. Modular Monolith has 5 negatives. Service-Based Architecture has 4 negatives. All exceed the 2-minimum threshold.
